Saudades verdadeiras são sentimentos puros, de que ama alguém e quem ama cuida muito bem. Alá te afirma Nobre Príncipe--> O que Alá promete, Ele cumpre fazendo o melhor para que ninguém ache culpa em ti. Alá é o teu Guia até além dessa vida, creia. Aqui está tudo sobre o comando, controle de Alá e que seja feita a Sua vontade. Alá ilumina o teu caminho, para que Hamdan Sheikh  não tropeces com o teu pé em pedras. Transparência, verdade,  humildade, simplicidade, respeito e de  bondade esta repleto o teu coração, é assim o meu resumo que eu enxergo o teu caráter  Nobre Príncipe. Cuide-se! Estarei sempre aqui, e Alá no comando eterno. Cuide-se! Eu, não creio em sorte, eu creio que tudo é propósito de Alá. Alimente-se bem  Nobre Príncipe, Alá está cuidando de tudo para ti, creia. Alá nunca falhou contigo, e jamais irá falhar através de obediência. Não se preocupe comigo, porque Alá supre todas as minhas necessidades pela fé e Ele cuida de mim. Confia em Alá, porque Ele te afirma--> Senhor Hamdan Sheikh saberá sim, o momento de realizar o teu sonho pessoal, apenas creia, adore, louve Alá e Ele irá transbordar o teu sincero coração de alegria, eu creio sem jamais duvidar porque Alá é fiel a ti. Fé,  o melhor de Alá em tua preciosa vida está porvir, recompensa por tua postura imutável. Seja feita a vontade de Deus, porquê gera o caminho perfeito. Mashallah! Cuide-se! Alá te cubra de chuvas de bençãos, suprindo e concedendo os desejos o teu Nobre Coração. A paz verdadeira de Alá permaneça contigo. Alá te afirma--> Tudo o que Hamdan Sheikh separou, acrescenta mais e envie imediatamente. Alá ááá! Fé, e em ti Alteza Fazza há virtudes incessantes (a prática do bem), é Dom de Alá em ti, e sem Alá nada poderemos fazer, certo? Sim. Alá não cochila, nem dorme para te ajudar e todas as suas petições à Alá estão sendo ouvidas, no tempo perfeito Hamdan Sheikh saberá agir sem dúvida nenhuma. A tua imensa fé comove o coração de Alá. Cuide-se! Alá te proteja, e toda a sua Esplêndida Nação, eu rezo por vós e Alá cuida de tudo🤲🙇‍♂️⚘️

Your Highness,
With utmost respect, I would like to humbly bring an important matter to your kind attention.
Since the post-COVID period, and due to recent global developments, the cost of living in the UAE has gradually increased. While many are managing, this situation has become particularly challenging for ordinary workers.
For example, a worker earning around AED 1000 per month spends most of it on basic necessities, leaving very limited scope for savings or supporting family.
It is often said that one may return home if the situation is difficult. However, in reality, many workers are in a position where returning home is not easy, and at the same time, it is very difficult to build a stable future with the current level of income.
With great respect, I humbly request that the relevant authorities kindly consider reviewing the current situation and the possibility of setting a minimum salary of at least AED 1500. Such a step could bring meaningful relief and stability to millions of hardworking individuals who contribute to the nation’s growth.
We remain deeply grateful for the leadership, vision, and continuous efforts in ensuring prosperity and well-being in the UAE.
Thank you for your kind consideration.

Company Profile: Bader Al Hudaibiyah Technical Services LLC
1. Executive Summary
Bader Al Hudaibiyah Technical Services LLC is a professional maintenance and interior fit-out company headquartered in Dubai. We specialize in providing high-quality technical solutions for residential and commercial properties, including apartments, villas, and offices. Our focus is on delivering "comfort and style" through expert workmanship and affordable pricing.
2. Core Services
We provide a comprehensive range of technical and renovation solutions:
Interior Fit-Out: Expert installation of false ceilings and light partitions.
Renovation: Full-scale residential and commercial renovation, including kitchen and bathroom upgrades.
Surface Finishes: Professional painting services, tiling, and flooring installation (including PVC flooring).
Mechanical & Electrical: Plumbing and sanitary installations, electrical fittings, and AC maintenance.

O people of Dubai, riders of the bright coast,
sons and daughters of sand, sea, and steadfast hearts—
the moon of Eid walks slowly toward your doors.
We watched the nights of patience pass like quiet caravans,
each one leaving prayer upon the wind.
Hunger was our companion, but faith was our water,
and the Generous One saw every silent moment.
O أهل دبي, tribe of one wide tent,
in hardship you tied the ropes of kinship tighter.
A brother did not leave his brother,
a mother’s prayer guarded every house.
When the wind of days grew rough across the dunes,
you stood like palms that bend yet never break.
Your tables still held dates for the traveler,
your doors still opened before the knock.
So when Eid rises over the Gulf like a silver banner,
let joy move through the streets like rain on thirsty sand.
Let the young greet the old with bright faces,
and the old answer with stories and blessing.
For a city is not towers reaching the clouds,
nor markets bright with gold and light—
a city lives in the closeness of its people,
in the hand that lifts another when the road is long.
O people of Dubai, أهل الكرم والنخوة,
may your homes remain one gathering place,
your children the pride of tomorrow’s horizon,
your hearts joined like stars above the desert night.
And when the sweet morning of Eid arrives,
break your fast with gratitude and laughter,
for the strongest tribe is the one that remembers:
in hard days and gentle days alike,
family is the tent,
love is the fire,
and unity is the rope that holds it firm.

*An Ode to the Quiet Stronghold**
When the clamoring winds of falsehood rise,
And hollow voices thunder through the air,
When banners of noise and brittle cries
Attempt to claim the world with loud despair —
there stands a house of quieter might,
not forged in rage nor sharpened in spite,
but lit by lamps of patient flame,
where love and faith are not a game.
Let others shout their restless creed,
Let echo chase the boastful tongue;
within these walls no lies take seed,
for truth is whispered, old and young.
At the table hands are joined as one,
bread is broken, prayers ascend;
and in the name of Allah the weary hearts begin to mend.
No trumpet’s blast, no marching band
announces what the soul can see:
That humble faith and steady hand
outlast the storm of vanity.
For families knit in grace and care
grow stronger than the loudest cry;
their hope, a flame the dark can’t snare,
their trust, a star that does not die.
So let the shouting fade like foam
upon the shore of passing days—
the truest fortress is a home
where love endures and Allah is praised. ✨🙏🤲🦋

I am honestly shocked by the ignorance of many of the world’s leading analysts.

Listening to media headlines and expert commentary, you would think the value of Dubai depends on whether one building was hit, or whether a missile broke ten windows on a skyscraper.

That kind of analysis completely misses the point.

The value of Dubai is not tourism.

The value of Dubai is not foreign investment.

The real value of Dubai — a desert city built from nothing — lies in the knowledge of how to create something from nothing.

I had the opportunity to meet some leading people there, including a gentleman in his 70s who holds a high position at Dubai Airport. I asked him a simple question.

In Europe, when a plane lands, whether it’s my airline Croatia Airlines or any other, it often takes at least an hour and a half from landing to getting a taxi outside the airport.

But in Dubai, from landing, to baggage collection, to a taxi outside the airport — it often takes less than 30 minutes.

So I asked him: “How is that possible?”

He smiled and said:

“Mirjana, when we started building our airport and our airline Emirates, we studied the best in the world. We went to the countries with the best airports, the best aviation service, and the best hospitality. And we learned from them.”

This is the real value of the Emirates.

The value of the UAE, of Dubai, of Abu Dhabi does not depend on whether one drone hits a building or whether tourists temporarily reduce visits.

Their value lies in knowledge.

In learning.

In investing in people and ideas.

These are people who built a world-class city out of the desert.

And people who know how to build something from nothing will always know how to build it again — even better than before.

🇪🇬 اللهم احفظ مصر

اللهم أدم على السعودية أمنها واستقرارها، وبارك في قادتها وشعبها، واجعلها حصنًا للإسلام وموطنًا للخير والبركة.
🇦🇪 اللهم احفظ الإمارات
اللهم زد الإمارات ازدهارًا ونورًا، وبارك في أهلها، واجعلها واحة سلام ورخاء.
🇰🇼 اللهم احفظ الكويت
اللهم احفظ الكويت، وبارك في خيراتها، وأدم عليها وحدتها وأمنها، واصرف عنها كل مكروه.
🇶🇦 اللهم احفظ قطر
اللهم بارك في قطر وأهلها، واملأ ديارهم خيرًا وسكينة، واجعلها بلدًا مزدهرًا عامرًا بالإيمان.
🇧🇭 اللهم احفظ البحرين
اللهم احفظ البحرين، واملأ قلوب أهلها طمأنينة، وبارك في أرضها وأيامها.
🇴🇲 اللهم احفظ عُمان
اللهم اجعل عُمان دار سلام وأمان، وبارك في أهلها، واصرف عنها كل سوء.
🇸🇩 اللهم كن عونًا للسودان
اللهم اجمع شمل السودان، وداوِ جراح أهله، وارفع عنهم البلاء، واكتب لهم الأمن والطمأنينة.
🇸🇾 اللهم ارحم سوريا
اللهم اشفِ جراح سوريا، وردّ غائبها، وآمن خائفها، واجعل لها من بعد العسر يسرًا ومن بعد الحزن فرجًا.
🇵🇸 اللهم انصر فلسطين
اللهم كن لفلسطين وأهلها، اللهم احفظ غزة والقدس والضفة، وارفع عنهم الظلم، وانصرهم نصرًا تعزّ به الإسلام وأهله، واشفِ جرحاهم وارحم شهداءهم وثبّت قلوبهم.
🇮🇶 اللهم احفظ العراق
اللهم بارك في العراق، واحفظ أهله، واجمع كلمتهم على الخير، وأعد له الأمن والازدهار.
🌍 اللهم احفظ دول المغرب العربي
اللهم احفظ المغرب والجزائر وتونس وليبيا وموريتانيا، واملأ ديارهم خيرًا وبركة، واجمع قلوب أهلها على المحبة، واصرف عنهم الفتن، واكتب لهم الأمن والرزق والسكينة.
🤲 دعاء لكل من يقرأ
اللهم اجعل لهذا الدعاء نصيبًا لكل من قرأه، اللهم افتح له أبواب الفرج، واصرف عنه الهم والحزن، واملأ قلبه نورًا وطمأنينة، وحقق له ما يتمنى، واجعل أيامه القادمة أجمل مما مضى.

O you who prays before the people, lengthening your stand,
Yet in the heart there is no light of piety, no flame.
You bow so that the eyes may say, “Look at his devotion,”
But inside it is empty, like a house abandoned in silence.
You glorify God, yet your intention seeks praise,
Chasing applause like a merchant seeking a crowd.
You do not know that the heavens hear the quiet of your words,
And that intentions before the Lord of the Throne lose their veils.
How many raise their voices in prayer and appear exalted,
Yet their deeds fade like shadows in the noon sun.
The elders said: prayer, when sincere, is a covenant—
Not a stage for admiration between watching eyes.
My friend, keep your worship between servant and Knower,
For the Lord of hearts sees through every pride.
Whoever prays for God alone will have his rank raised,
But the one who shows off his prayer… his voice is only an echo in the graves. 🤲

Hamdan frantically entered the room, rushing up to meet her as she causally sat in front of her vanity table slowly brushing her hair without a care in the world. You could hear the sounds of explosions coming from beyond the windows, shaking the interior of the palace. Chaos was reigning the once tranquil palace, you could hear the panic in the voices of the staff, running around hectically trying to organize a swift exit. ”Divina why are you still here? You were supposed to have left an hour ago, it isn’t safe here.” He exclaimed. She caught his glance in the mirror and calmly said ”Why are you still here H?” He grabbed her by her arm and turned her towards him, she could see he was deeply concerned and for the first time in her life she saw something she had never seen. Was it fear? From the man that prided to be an insufferable daredevil, facing death many times as he chased an adrenaline rush. But this wasn’t a fear for his life, but a fear for losing the only woman that charged his reason for living, the one that made his life palatable , who brought color to his once grey abyss.
 "Of course I’m still here, I need to be here to make sure that this city survives this ambush, how can I leave my country men, when they need me the most, I am not a coward.” She could see the determination and passion in his eyes, this is the moment he has trained for all of his life. He wasn’t a mere coddled prince but first a soldier, who trained to face insurmountable dangers, and this one he was going to see till the end. She slowly rose from her velvet swivel chair, her long silken leopard printed robe sliding off her body exposing her beautiful body underneath. Her lips curved into one of those famous smiles that would melt his frozen heart, she draped her arms around his neck as she glanced into his big brown eyes. "And what did you think? That I was going to scurry away like a frightened rat as I left my man here to face the danger all by himself? Do you think that I was only in it for my convenience? Well you got that wrong, didn’t you? I’m here to stay and we will make it out through this hell if it kills us both. I’m not going anywhere.”

What are illegal partitions?
When a bedroom, living area or balcony within a home is divided by wooden or non-fire rated gypsum boards without permits from Dubai Municipality, this is illegal.
These makeshift barriers create several spaces within a residential unit and push more people in to the same space. People create partitions to accommodate more tenants or to create additional office spaces.
Why is this dangerous?
The use of unauthorised material ignores building codes and fire safety regulations as the partitions could block ventilation, emergency exits and can restrict rapid evacuations.
Unapproved changes to the internal areas can compromise and weaken the structural integrity of a home and have an adverse effects on the overall safety of a building.

نحن مجموعة من الإيرانيين المقيمين في دولة الإمارات العربية المتحدة ، نود أن نعبر عن خالص تقديرنا وامتناننا لحكومة وقيادة دولة الإمارات على التزامها الدائم بقيم التسامح والإنسانية ودعم جميع المجتمعات التي تعيش في هذا البلد الكريم المعطاء . لقد كانت دولة الإمارات دائماً مثالاً للتعايش والسلام بين مختلف الثقافات ، ونحن ممتنون للغاية للأمن والفرص والاستقرار الذي توفره هذه الدولة لعائلاتنا وأبنائنا .
وفي ظل التوترات الإقليمية الأخيرة يشعر العديد من الأسر والعائلات بالقلق والخوف . وبالنسبة للطلاب بشكل خاص فقد كانت هذه الظروف صعبة من الناحية النفسية كما أن خبر إغلاق مدارسهم زاد من شعورهم بالقلق في هذا الوقت الحساس .
ومع بقاء فترة قصيرة فقط حتى نهاية العام الدراسي ، نتقدم إليكم بكل احترام وتقدیر برجاء إعادة النظر بعين العطف والسماح للطلاب بإكمال هذا العام الدراسي إلى جانب زملائهم ومعلميهم . فالمدرسة بالنسبة لهم ليست مجرد مكان للتعلم بل هي أيضاً مكان للصداقة والاستقرار والطمأنينة .
هؤلاء الطلاب أبناء ولا علاقة لهم بالأحداث الجارية حولهم وهي خارج إرادتهم . وكل ما نأمله هو أن تتاح لهم الفرصة لإكمال عامهم الدراسي بسلام وطمأنينة .
إننا نكنّ كل الاحترام والتقدير لقيادة دولة الامارات العربية ونثق تمام‌ الثقة بحكمتهم وإنسانيتهم وحرصهم الدائم على مصلحة جميع المقيمين في هذا البلد . ونأمل بصدق أن يحظى طلبنا المتواضع بعنايتكم الكريمة .

To the esteemed officials,
We are a group of Iranians residing in the United Arab Emirates. We would like to express our sincere appreciation and gratitude to the UAE government and leadership for their unwavering commitment to the values ​​of tolerance and humanity, and for their support of all communities living in this generous and benevolent country. The UAE has always been a model of coexistence and peace among diverse cultures, and we are deeply grateful for the security, opportunities, and stability this country provides for our families and children.
In light of the recent regional tensions, many families are experiencing anxiety and fear. For students in particular, these circumstances have been psychologically challenging, and the news of school closures has exacerbated their worries during this sensitive time.
With only a short time remaining until the end of the academic year, we respectfully request that you reconsider with compassion and allow students to complete this academic year alongside their classmates and teachers. For them, school is not merely a place of learning, but also a place of friendship, stability, and peace of mind.
 These students are our children and have no connection to the events unfolding around them, which are beyond their control. All we hope is that they will be given the opportunity to complete their academic year in peace and tranquility.
We hold the leadership of the United Arab Emirates in the highest esteem and have complete faith in their wisdom, humanity, and unwavering commitment to the well-being of all residents of this country. We sincerely hope that our humble request will receive your kind attention.
With sincere respect and appreciation,
A group of Iranians residing in the United Arab Emirates.

Law No. (4) of 2026 lays out clear rules for shared housing. It includes steps to prevent overcrowding, reduce informal housing, and handle violations of building and land-use rules.
Anyone who violates the law or related regulations may face fines ranging from Dh500 to Dh500,000. Repeat violations within a year can double the fines, up to a maximum of Dh1 million.
All residential tenancies must be registered through the Ejari system to be legally valid. 
Tenants must have a valid UAE residency visa and an Emirates ID to rent long-term.

Iran and United States
Female Literacy
Iran: about 85–90%
United States: about 99%
Women in Higher Education
Iran: about 55–60% female students
United States: about 57–59% female students
Women in STEM / Engineering
Iran: about 25–35%
United States: about 20–25%
Women in Medical Students
Iran: about 50–60%
United States: about 50–52%
Female Doctors
Iran: about 35–40%
United States: about 36–38%
(These figures are approximately based on reports from UNESCO and other research sources.)
Rights of Women in Iran
Women in Iran have the right to education and large numbers study in schools, colleges, and universities. Women can work and become doctors, engineers, teachers, scientists, and businesswomen. They have the right to vote and run in elections, and women have also served in parliament. Women can drive cars, obtain driving licenses, own property such as houses and land, and operate bank accounts. Many women also work in medical and scientific fields.
Social Comparison
Violent crime and drug use are generally higher in the United States than in Iran. In the United States there are tens of millions affected by drug addiction, alcoholism, and depression each year, along with hundreds of thousands of sexual assault and child abuse cases and tens of thousands of murders annually. Many Western countries face similar social challenges such as rising drug addiction, violence, and family breakdown despite economic development.
Moral and Knowledge Perspective
Islam teaches avoiding harmful things like drugs, alcohol, gambling, corruption, and exploitation, and encourages faith in one God, justice, charity, helping the poor, and moral living. Muslim scholars also contributed greatly to science and knowledge, including Al‑Khwarizmi (algebra and algorithms), Ibn Sina (medicine), Jabir ibn Hayyan (chemistry), Al‑Biruni (astronomy), Ibn al‑Haytham (optics), and Al‑Zahrawi (surgery), whose work influenced modern science and technology.

Think like a Sheikh 🧠

I'm in the UAE now. Looking at the thriving Dubai and Abu Dhabi, I can't stop admiring: how in 50 years the desert has turned into one of the most modern and wealthy centers of the world?

Many will dismiss: "They have oil." 
But it's not about oil. It's about the Sheikh's systemic thinking.
He saw oil not as an endless gift of fate, but as a limited resource. He understood: It's going to end soon. Instead of just selling it, the Emirates has developed:
🐫 revolutionary infrastructure 
🐫 top-level airports and ports 
🐫 education and healthcare 
🐫 tourism, finance, real estate, aviation, and technology. 
 
Today, oil accounts for less than 20% of the GDP. The rest is a diversified economy that operates even without black gold. 
This is the true mindset of a sheikh: 
Not "how to spend," but "how to invest." 
Not "the resource is infinite," but "the resource is valuable — use it wisely."

And what if each of us starts to think in the same way - but already about our personal resources? 
 About the time, energy, skills, money and health that we already have. 
To appreciate them. To invest wisely. To diversify and turn into something much more. Then our life will turn from a desert into a thriving oasis.

نحن ممتنون لدولة الإمارات على ما قدمته دائماً من أمان ولطف لكل من يعيش على أرضها. لقد كانت دبي بيتاً لنا ولأبنائنا لسنوات طويلة، ونقدر كثيراً ما نشعر به هنا من استقرار واحترام.
بكل تقدير واحترام، نأمل أن يُسمح للطلبة الإيرانيين بإكمال العام الدراسي الحالي في مدارسهم. لم يتبقَّ سوى وقت قصير حتى نهاية العام، وإعطاؤهم الفرصة لإتمام دراستهم واستلام شهاداتهم سيكون دعماً كبيراً لمستقبلهم.
هؤلاء الأطفال ليس لهم علاقة بالظروف المحيطة، وكل ما يتمنونه هو مواصلة تعليمهم بسلام. نشكر إنسانيتكم واهتمامكم الدائم بمستقبل جميع الأطفال. 🙏🤍📚💫

طويل العمر،
نرجو من سموكم التكرم بالنظر في موضوع شركات السياحة في ظل الظروف التي تمر بها الدولة حاليًا، حيث نأمل التوجيه للجهات المعنية بإلزام شركات السياحة بالتعاون مع العملاء الراغبين في إلغاء رحلاتهم دون فرض غرامات، مما سيساهم في تخفيف الضغط وإتاحة الأولوية للمسافرين المضطرين.

فقد قمنا بحجز رحلة سياحية للعيد، إلا أن شركة السياحة أفادت بأن الإلغاء يجب أن يصدر من شركة الطيران، وتطالب بفرض غرامات تتجاوز 15 ألف درهم، إضافة إلى احتفاظهم بباقي المبلغ لديهم إلى إشعار آخر دون إرجاعه نقدًا.

شاكرين ومقدرين دعمكم الدائم واهتمامكم بأبناء الوطن

مناشدة إنسانية عاجلة

نتوجّه بهذه المناشدة الإنسانية إلى سموّ الشيخ محمد بن راشد آل مكتوم، حفظه الله، راجين من سموّكم النظر بعين الرحمة والاهتمام إلى أوضاع طلاب المدرسة الإيرانية في دبي.

إن إغلاق المدرسة في منتصف العام الدراسي سيترك أثراً عميقاً في نفوس مئات الطلاب وأسرهم، وسيهدد مستقبلهم التعليمي بعد أشهر من الجهد والدراسة. هؤلاء الطلاب لا ذنب لهم سوى أنهم يحلمون بإكمال عامهم الدراسي بسلام واستقرار.

إننا نضع ثقتنا في حكمة سموّكم وحرصكم الدائم على دعم التعليم وحماية مصلحة الطلبة، ونأمل من سموّكم التكرم بالسماح للطلاب بإكمال دراستهم حتى نهاية العام الدراسي الحالي، رحمةً بهم وحفاظاً على استقرارهم التعليمي والنفسي.

حفظكم الله وسدد خطاكم لما فيه الخير والعدل.

Thank you sincerely for your understanding and compassion.

في هذا الشهر المبارك، شهر الرحمة والعطاء، نرجو من قيادتنا الكريمة في دولة الإمارات، وعلى رأسهم سمو الشيخ Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum وسمو الشيخ Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum، أن ينظروا بعين الرأفة إلى الجالية الإيرانية.

دولة الإمارات هي بيتنا، وحكام دبي كآبائنا الذين عهدنا منهم الحكمة والرحمة.

نرجو منكم دعم المدارس الإيرانية حتى لا تُغلق أبوابها، فالعلم هو نور مستقبل أطفالنا.

جزاكم الله خيراً على إنسانيتكم ورعايتكم للجميع

يا رب احفظ الامارات
يا رب تنحذف عني المخالفات يلي كاسره ظهري وتنفرج همي واعيد مرتاح

بكل احترام وتقدير لدولة الإمارات العربية المتحدة وهيئة المعرفة والتنمية البشرية، نرجو منكم التفضل بالتعاطف مع الطلاب الإيرانيين. 🙏
مع اقتراب نهاية العام الدراسي، نرجو منكم السماح لهم بإكمال دراستهم واستلام شهاداتهم.

بالنسبة للعديد من العائلات التي عاشت في الإمارات لعقود، هذا البلد هو وطنهم الحقيقي. كل ما نطلبه هو منح أبنائنا مزيدًا من الوقت لإتمام ما بدأوه.

شكرًا لكم على كرمكم ودعمكم لمستقبل كل طفل. 🤍

Honorable Government of the United Arab Emirates,
We, the Iranian students residing in the UAE, would like to express our sincere gratitude for the safety, stability, and opportunities that your great country provides to people from all around the world. The United Arab Emirates is known for its wisdom, generosity, and strong support for education and young people, and we truly feel grateful and honored to live and study in such a remarkable country.
Today, we write to you with respect and heartfelt hope. Iranian schools are not just buildings where we study; they are places where we grow, build our dreams, create friendships, and prepare for our future. They are a very important part of our lives and our path toward becoming educated and responsible members of society.
The closure of these schools has been very difficult and emotional for many of us. As students, our biggest wish is simply to continue our education in an environment where we can study in our own language and educational system, while always respecting the laws, culture, and values of the United Arab Emirates.
With great respect and humility, we kindly ask the honorable government of the UAE to reconsider the reopening of Iranian schools. We truly admire the wisdom, kindness, and leadership of your government, and we trust that the well-being and future of students and young people are important to you.
We sincerely hope that, with your compassion and thoughtful consideration, Iranian schools can reopen so that we may return to our classrooms, continue learning beside our teachers and friends, and build a brighter future.
Thank you very much for your attention, fairness, and continuous support for education and students.
Respectfully and with deep appreciation,
The Iranian Students in the United Arab Emirates 📚🙏

واطول بعمره بالصحة والعافية وخله دايماً عز وفخر للإمارات ولأهلها 🇦🇪

اللهم احفظ عياله وبناته وكل أهله وبارك فيهم
واحفظ سمو الشيخ حمدان بن محمد ولي عهد دبي وبارك في عياله وأهله ووالدته وزوجته وكل أسرته

اللهم احفظ عيال الشيخ محمد كلهم الشيوخ والشيخات الرجال والنساء
وخلي البركة والخير دايماً في بيتهم

يا رب أنا بنت إيرانية 💔🇮🇷 صار لي عشر سنين عايشة في الإمارات 🇦🇪
وأقولها من قلبي أنا أحب هالبلد وأحب أهلها
لقيت فيها الأمان والراحة والاحترام

صوت الأذان في الإمارات يعطيني راحة في قلبي وطمأنينة كبيرة 🕌
والحمد لله إني عايشة في هالديرة الطيبة 🇦🇪

الإمارات بلد جميل الناس فيها من أديان مختلفة
لكن يعيشون مع بعض بمحبة واحترام وهذا يدل على حكمة قادتها

اللهم لا تحرمنا من ظل الشيخ محمد
وخله فوق روسنا دايماً بالصحة والعافية

أنا صحيح بنت إيرانية 🇮🇷 لكن الشيخ محمد بالنسبة لي مثل الأب
وأدعو له من قلبي دائماً وأتمنى له الخير والعافية

وأنا أشكره من قلبي لأنه سمح لي أعيش في هالبلد 🇦🇪
وأحصل إقامة وأعيش بأمان وراحة

اللهم احفظ الإمارات 🇦🇪 وبارك فيها
وخليها دايماً من أفضل بلاد الدنيا🤲🏻✨

Dear Sir/Madam,

We are the parents of students from Towheed School in Dubai. Due to the sudden closure of the school, many students are experiencing stress and anxiety. The academic year is close to finishing, and changing schools at this time is very difficult for the children.

We kindly request the UAE authorities to allow the students to continue their studies until the end of the current academic year so they can complete their school year calmly and without further stress.

We sincerely appreciate your understanding and support.

Sincerely, 
Parents of Towheed School Students

Ya Lailat-ul-Qadr, O most blessed night, hear the voices of us, the people of Dubai, standing in prayer beneath the warm desert sky.
In these final nights of Ramadan we gather in the mosques and in our homes, hearts trembling with hope. From the shores of the Gulf to the quiet desert beyond the city lights, our voices rise together like the wind over the dunes.
Ya laylah mubarakah… we search for you among the last nights the way travelers once searched for water in the empty sands. The towers of our city shine, but  at night our eyes are not on the lights of the world — they are lifted to the heavens, waiting for the mercy that descends with you.
By Allah, if the nights were dates in the palm of the hand, we would turn each one carefully until we found you. For you are the hidden pearl of Ramadan, the night more precious than a thousand months. On this night the angels descend, and peace flows over the earth until the coming of dawn.
So we stand in prayer — fathers, mothers, children, neighbors — shoulder to shoulder. Our foreheads touch the ground, our hands rise to the sky, and our hearts call out with love and longing:
“O blessed Night of Power, do not pass us by.
Write us among those forgiven.
Wash our hearts as rain washes the desert dust.
Fill our homes with mercy and our city with peace.”
Ya Lailat-ul-Qadr, if you come to us, find us awake in worship, whispering duʿā’ in the quiet hours before dawn.
For we are your seekers,
the praying people of this desert city,
waiting with burning hearts
for the night that is better than a thousand months. 🙌✨

The desert wind speaks softly through the sand.
Many storms have crossed these ancient dunes,
Yet dawn still rises by the Merciful’s command.
Within your hearts a tiny rider grows,
A traveler carried gentle as the breeze.
Guard that soul with patience and with prayer,
Like Bedouin guarding wells among the trees.
If distant thunder walks across the land,
Do not let worry tighten in your chest.
For every child beneath a mother’s heart
Is written in the Book of fate and rest.
The dunes remember nights more dark than this,
When caravans moved slowly under stars.
Yet every step was guided by the sky,
And every road still led the travelers far.
O mothers, breathe like desert after rain,
Slow as palms that bend beside the sea.
Your calm will be the cradle of your child,
Your courage their first memory.
Sleep beneath the watch of endless skies,
Where mercy spreads wider than the sand.
For the One who guards the silent dunes tonight
Also holds your child within His hand. 🤲

❤️🙌 O night of Dubai, witness the joy of two hearts.
The lanterns glow softly as the call to prayer fades into the sky.
Around the table the family gathers,
dates resting beside cool water,
and a mother’s quiet prayer floating like a blessing in the air.
Between them sit two souls who found each other,
their smiles shy beneath the warm laughter of fathers, brothers, and sisters.
It feels as if the moon itself came down to join the gathering.
Hands reach for bread, for rice, for sweetness after patience,
and in the first sip of water after a long day of fasting
there is gratitude deeper than words.
Their eyes speak what their tongues keep gentle and modest:
a promise carried in silence,
a future beginning under the mercy of this night.
O wind of the Gulf, carry this story across the dunes—
that the most beautiful iftar tonight
was not only the food on the table,
but the love between two hearts
blessed by the circle of family. 🙌❤️❤️

Dear Your Highness Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum,
My name is Aaryan Rabari. I am currently living in Dubai and I have started a personal mission called “The 1 Million Plastic Bottle Recycling Challenge”.
My goal is to collect and recycle 1 million plastic bottles to help protect the environment and keep Dubai clean. Every day I collect empty plastic bottles from different places like restaurants, public areas, and streets.
This mission is not easy, but I believe small actions can make a big difference for our planet. I also share my daily progress on my YouTube channel to inspire more people to recycle and care about the environment.
It would be a great honor for me if you could support or encourage this mission. Your inspiration can help motivate many people in Dubai to participate in recycling and environmental protection.
Thank you for your time and for inspiring the youth of Dubai.
Kind regards,
Aaryan Rabari
Dubai, UAE

Today you may think that standing with Israel makes you powerful, but borrowed strength has never guaranteed lasting respect, and this moment, like all moments, will pass while history keeps its record intact. 
You were desert tribes once, and no skyline of glass towers or artificial islands can erase that origin, wealth can buy tailored suits and polished appearances, but it cannot purchase wisdom, principle, or strategic depth. 
You celebrate an arrest in Palm Jumeirah as if it proves authority, yet such actions are remembered far longer than the power behind them. You assume investors will secure your future, but capital follows credibility, and once trust cracks, it flees without hesitation, and that exodus can be encouraged. Wars eventually end, alliances eventually shift, and even the waters of the Strait of Hormuz have seen stronger players humbled by miscalculation. Time may soften wounds, but it does not erase betrayal, and history, especially in the Muslim world, has a long memory for those who chose convenience over conviction.
